title: 7 Herbal Creams For Joint Pain
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great way to relieve joint pain, and they work by using natural ingredients that have anti-inflammatory properties. When we apply these creams to our skin, the active ingredients are absorbed into the body, where they can help reduce swelling and ease pain. Herbal teas, on the other hand, work in a similar way, but through a different route. By drinking herbal teas, we're getting the same active ingredients into our body, but through our digestive system.  Let's take a look at three examples of herbs that are commonly used to relieve joint pain. Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When we consume curcumin, it can help reduce joint pain and inflammation. Another herb that's often used is Zingiber officinale, or ginger, which has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help ease pain and stiffness. Capsicum annuum, or cayenne pepper, also contains a compound called capsaicin, which can help reduce pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P.  Using herbal creams or teas to relieve joint pain can bring many benefits to our lives. For one, it can help us avoid taking pain medication, which can have unpleasant side effects. Herbal creams and teas are also often less expensive than over-the-counter pain medication, and they can be just as effective. Additionally, using herbal remedies can help us feel more in control of our health, and more connected to nature. By incorporating herbal creams and teas into our daily routine, we can start to feel more comfortable and confident in our bodies, and better equipped to manage our joint pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains the active constituents curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce joint pain and swelling. Curcumin, the main active ingredient, blocks enzymes that cause inflammation and damage to joint tissues, while demethoxycurcumin has antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress. Bisdemethoxycurcumin also has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and stiffness. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, curcuma longa creams can help alleviate joint pain and promote overall joint health.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for joint pain
remedy_procedure: Mix 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa) and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the mixture.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax is melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely to form a solid cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called gingerol, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. Gingerol blocks the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation in the joints. The creams also contain a compound called shogaol, which has antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the joints. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of gingerol and shogaol in Zingiber officinale creams may help reduce joint pain and stiffness by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy joint function. When applied topically, these compounds can penetrate deep into the skin and reach the affected joints, providing targeted relief from joint pain.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for joint pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder, 2 tablespoons of ginger powder, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the turmeric and ginger powders to the melted oil and mix well. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Add 2 tablespoons of distilled water to the mixture and mix until smooth. Stir in the vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your ginger cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum creams contains capsaicin, a bioactive constituent that helps reduce joint pain. Capsaicin blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. This blocks the pain, providing relief from joint pain and inflammation. Capsaicin also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and redness in the affected joints. The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of capsaicin in Capsicum annuum creams make it a potential treatment option for joint pain sufferers.
remedies.image_alt: Capsicum annuum herbal creams for joint pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Capsicum annuum, 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Grind the Capsicum annuum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the ground Capsicum annuum powder and vitamin E oil to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, store the cream in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams contains bioactive constituents like thymol, tannins, and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and ease joint pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body. The tannins in Arnica montana have a soothing effect, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in damaged tissues. The flavonoids in the cream have antioxidant properties, which help protect the joints from further damage and promote healthy cell growth. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Arnica montana creams can help alleviate joint pain and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Arnica montana herbal creams for joint pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of arnica oil, and 10 drops of arnica essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the shea butter and stir until melted. Remove from heat.
Add the arnica oil and essential oil. Stir well. Pour the mixture into a mixing bowl.
Let the mixture cool and thicken. Pour into containers and store in a cool place. Apply to affected joints as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and triterpenoid saponins. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and pain in joints. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, blocks the activity of an enzyme called 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which breaks down cortisol in the body. By blocking this enzyme, glycyrrhizin allows cortisol levels to remain high, reducing inflammation and pain in the joints. The saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams also have a soothing effect on the skin, making it a popular remedy for joint pain and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for joint pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 1 teaspoon of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add glycerin, glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and vitamin E oil to the melted beeswax. Stir until well combined.
Slowly add the distilled water to the mixture, stirring constantly until a smooth cream forms.
Remove from heat, let cool, and pour into containers for storage and use as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams contains compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, particularly achillin and alantolactone, and polyacetylenes. These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in joints. The creams also contain flavonoids, which are antioxidants that combat oxidative stress and promote healing in the affected area. The anti-inflammatory properties of Achillea millefolium creams are thought to be due to the inhibition of COX-2 and LOX enzymes, which are involved in the production of inflammatory mediators. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Achillea millefolium creams may provide relief from joint pain.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for joint pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until the beeswax melts.
Add the dried Achillea millefolium flowers to the melted oil and beeswax mixture. Let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Add the shea butter and vitamin E oil to the strained mixture. Stir well and pour the mixture into small containers. Let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contain bioactive constituents like vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-sitosterol, a plant sterol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in joints. The gel's high water content and glycoproteins also promote cartilage regeneration and hydration, soothing dry and irritated joints. Furthermore, aloe vera's antioxidants and enzymes help break down and remove waste products that can cause joint pain and inflammation. By promoting healthy joint tissue and reducing inflammation, Aloe barbadensis creams can provide relief from joint pain and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for joint pain
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil in the double boiler over low heat.
Add the Aloe barbadensis gel to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove from heat and stir in the vitamin E oil. Let cool until solidified.
Once solid, scoop the mixture into containers and store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea might help reduce joint pain. Ginger tea, for example, has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease swelling and discomfort in joints. Turmeric tea also contains a compound called curcumin, which has been shown to reduce joint pain and inflammation. Some people find that these teas help alleviate their joint pain.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can be a natural way to ease joint pain, consuming them daily can have mixed effects. Some teas, like ginger and turmeric, are generally considered safe in moderation, but others, like willow bark, contain salicylates that can cause issues in large doses. Moderation is key to avoiding potential problems.
faq_timeline: The time it takes for herbal teas to show results in joint pain varies from person to person. Some people feel relief within a few days, while others may take a week or two. It's common for herbal teas like ginger or turmeric to start reducing pain and inflammation within 7-10 days, but consistency is key to seeing long-term benefits.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for joint pain can be most effective in the morning or early afternoon. This is because it can help increase circulation and reduce inflammation throughout the day. As you go about your daily activities, the tea's soothing effects can continue to provide relief from joint pain.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for joint pain. Some herbal teas, like ginkgo biloba and feverfew, can slow down blood clotting, which can be a problem if you're taking blood-thinning medications. St. John's Wort can also affect how well your body absorbs other medications, including pain relievers.
faq_pregnant: When it comes to drinking herbal teas for joint pain while pregnant, it's best to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, are generally considered safe, but others, like those containing foxglove or pennyroyal, can be problematic. Always check the ingredients and avoid anything that might harm you or your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's best to check the ingredients of herbal teas for joint pain. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, are usually safe. However, others like turmeric and ginger in large amounts may affect milk production or baby's health. Always read labels and use small amounts to be sure.
title_2: 7 Natural Treatments for Joint Pain in Runners Using Creams
